patterns.reminder: Looks like a real Brazilian CPF. deEHR uses synthetic test data only (no real PHI in the repo), and CPF/CNS must never be written on-chain (public, immutable, un-erasable). Replace with synthetic data.
Looks like a Brazilian CNS (Cartao Nacional de Saude, 15 digits). Use synthetic data only; never put a CNS or any identifier on-chain. If this is a hash or timestamp, disregard.
Embedded private key material. Keys (patient signing keys, ICP-Brasil certs) live in an HSM/KMS and must never be committed, logged, or exported. Load from the secret manager.

Possible key export / seed handling. Custodied keys must never leave the HSM boundary (no export, log, serialize, or return), and progressive custody exposes no seed phrase to patients. Confirm this stays inside custody.
panic / unwrap / expect in contract code: a WASM trap on attacker-influenced input is a DoS. Use require! / sc_panic! with a checked condition, or handle the error. Test-only code can disregard.
